page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-16-lssd is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 16 vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing dedicated CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 128 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Local storage is bundled via a 402 GB NVMe SSD, while GPU acceleration is absent.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU execution, as evidenced by stress-ng single-core and Geekbench text processing results landing in the top 25%. Multi-core operations, software compilation, and database operations perform at average levels. However, the server exhibits a notable bottleneck in uncached memory bandwidth, with large-block read-only and write bandwidth testing in the weak and poor categories respectively. Cryptographic and vector workloads benefit from hardware-accelerated instruction sets including AVX-512 and AES.

Given its high memory density and local NVMe storage, this instance is well-suited for memory-intensive workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and text processing pipelines. The inclusion of a local SSD provides low-latency temporary storage, enhancing its utility for database operations. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers a dense memory profile and dedicated compute resources without the overhead of GPU hardware, though workloads requiring sustained high-throughput memory writes may encounter performance trade-offs.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-16-lssd is a memory-optimized virtual server configured with 16 vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM, delivering a high 8.0 GB per vCPU resource density. Powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor with x86_64 architecture, it features 8 physical cores with hyper-threading and dedicated CPU allocation. The hardware configuration includes a local 402 GB NVMe SSD for low-latency storage, but lacks GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance and text processing capabilities, alongside average multi-core and database execution. However, uncached memory write bandwidth is a documented bottleneck, performing in the bottom 10%. This server is highly suitable for memory-intensive applications, text processing, and caching databases that benefit from dedicated compute resources and local SSD storage without requiring massive memory write throughput.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-16-lssd is a memory-optimized server with 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 402 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core CPU performance for memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC compute cores, high RAM density, and integrated local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: 16 vCPUs and 128 GB RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core memory density
Dedicated AMD EPYC 9B14 processor with AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration
Integrated 402 GB local NVMe SSD for low-latency storage operations
Strong single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
Suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and text processing workloads
